# What’s Currently Happening in Precision Measurement? (Q1 2024)

###### Related topics:

#### Accelerate Productivity in 2025

###### Reignite Growth Despite the Global Slowdown

What if the instruments you employ to assess the specifics of your goods could also forecast the trajectory of your company? Within the dynamic field of precise measurement, quantum technologies are delivering profound performance improvements. This article analyzes the advances and ramifications in precise measurement, a field that has subtly advanced everything from manufacturing to space exploration.  From the latest industry events to important partnerships in the field, this quarterly precision measurement news brief for January, February, and March 2024 provides a comprehensive snapshot of what is happening in the global precision measurement industry today.

## Key Takeaways

## How We Researched and Where this Data is from

This article is fueled by insights from the StartUs Insights [Discovery Platform](https://www.startus-insights.com/startus-insights-platform/), a comprehensive resource tracking more than 3.7 million startups and scaleups globally. Our platform monitors over 20,000 technology trends, covering 100K feeds across various industries.

For this piece, we’ve leveraged the platform to analyze 1000 of the most recent and impactful news evolving around precision measurement systems. Further, our platform allows you to dig deeper into precision measurement solutionsand investigate other topics based on your preferences. Interested in exploring the latest news, developments, innovations, and breakthroughs relevant to your business? [Book a demo](#) with us today and discover the full potential of our platform for your strategic needs.

## What’s Currently Happening in Precision Measurement?

### Leading Edge Technology: Quantum Lasers and Sensors

The use of lasers for precise measurement and the development of quantum sensors recently made news. At the forefront are businesses like Carl Zeiss India and Cross Precision Measurement. They are showcasing state-of-the-art metrology systems that are shattering previous records for accuracy. A strategic change that may have an impact on the industry is indicated by [Hexagon AB’s sale of its Tesa PMI business](https://hexagon.com/company/newsroom/press-releases/2023/hexagon-agrees-sale-of-non-core-business-area) to Hangzhou Great Star Industrial.

The debut of [Hitachi High-Tech’s dark field wafer defect inspection system](https://www.hitachi.com/New/cnews/month/2023/12/231206.html) and the development of new lasers for digital holography are two examples of the constant drive for innovation. This technical vanguard enables the industry to leapfrog into the future, not only make tools sharper.

### Impact on Industry: From Defense to Manufacturing

These developments have far-reaching effects. Precision measuring technologies are transforming production processes and quality control across industries. For instance, the defense industry expects navigation and targeting system improvements. In the technological sector, there is a noticeable upsurge in fields where precision metrology is essential, such as the industrial metaverse and 3D-IC packages.

With [HYDROCOW receiving an extra $6 million in funding](https://agfundernews.com/hydrocow-project-bags-6m-to-make-whey-protein-from-co2-hydrogen-its-high-risk-high-reward) for its precision fermentation tech, the investing community’s faith in the field’s potential is evident. This also demonstrates the favorable attitude surrounding these technological advancements. It’s not all plain sailing, though. The shadow cast by Spectris’ stock price’s 52-week low serves as a reminder that market difficulties still exist.

### Market Dynamics: A Hub for Mergers and Acquisitions

Just as dynamic as the technologies themselves is the market environment. As businesses like [Stellus and Summit Park make calculated decisions to acquire organizations](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20240109912949/en/Stellus-Capital-Management-LLC-Provides-Unitranche-Financing-in-Support-of-Summit-Park%E2%80%99s-Acquisition-of-Michelli-Weighing-Measurement) like Michelli Weighing & Measurement, mergers, acquisitions, and divestitures are frequent occurrences. These agreements allow businesses to focus more narrowly and capitalize on their fundamental strengths.

### The Quantum Dilemma: Maintaining Lead

The message is clear for managers of innovation and business leaders: leading means adapting. Integrating new technologies into current systems and guaranteeing their dependability under varied circumstances are essential for remaining ahead of the curve in this quickly changing area. It’s a challenging task, but individuals who can pull it off will find themselves leading a changing industry.

### Future Prospective: Immediate, Intermediate, and Extended Effects

Businesses also need to manage the short-term integration of these new technologies with current workflows. The emphasis switches to training and development in the middle term to make sure the staff is prepared to handle new systems. Investing in research and development to create exclusive solutions that give a competitive edge is the obvious long-term objective.

# The Future of Precision Measurement

**![](https://www.mitutoyo.com/webfoo/wp-content/uploads/7001B-Digital-Measuring-Tools.jpg)Introduction to Precision Measurement Technology**

![](https://www.mitutoyo.com/webfoo/wp-content/uploads/7001B-Digital-Measuring-Tools.jpg)

Precision measurement technology has evolved significantly over the years, driven by the need for greater accuracy and efficiency in various industries. From traditional mechanical tools to advanced digital systems, these technologies play a critical role in ensuring quality and precision in manufacturing, healthcare, and other fields. 

**Recent Advancements in Precision Measurement Tools** 

**Digital and Smart Technologies**

Recent years have seen the rise of digital and smart technologies in precision measurement. Digital micrometers, calipers, and other tools offer higher accuracy and ease of use. Smart systems can store data, perform calculations, and integrate with other software, streamlining workflows and reducing human error. 

 

**Enhanced Accuracy and Efficiency**

Modern measurement tools are designed to provide unparalleled accuracy. Innovations such as laser scanning and 3D measurement technologies allow for precise measurements down to the micron level. These advancements not only improve the quality of products but also increase efficiency, reducing the time and cost associated with quality control processes. 

 

**![](https://www.mitutoyo.com/webfoo/wp-content/uploads/7007-Micrometers-2.jpg)Overview of the Mitutoyo QuantuMike**:

![](https://www.mitutoyo.com/webfoo/wp-content/uploads/7007-Micrometers-2.jpg)

**Key Features of the Mitutoyo QuantuMike**

Mitutoyo QuantuMike is a standout in the field of precision measurement. It boasts several key features, including a high-accuracy mechanism that ensures consistent measurements. The QuantuMike also offers a faster measurement process, reducing the time needed to obtain precise readings. Its ergonomic design and robust construction make it a reliable tool for professionals. 

 

**Applications of the Mitutoyo QuantuMike**

The QuantuMike is widely used in industries requiring high precision, such as aerospace, automotive, and manufacturing. Its ability to deliver accurate measurements quickly makes it ideal for quality control processes. In addition, its digital readout feature allows for easy data recording and analysis, enhancing productivity in various applications. 

![](https://www.mitutoyo.com/webfoo/wp-content/uploads/7002-Basic-Measuring.jpg)

![](https://www.mitutoyo.com/webfoo/wp-content/uploads/7002-Basic-Measuring.jpg)

**The Impact of Advanced Measurement Tools on Various Industries**

**Manufacturing Industry**

In the manufacturing sector, precision measurement tools like the Mitutoyo QuantuMike are indispensable. They ensure that components meet strict specifications, reducing waste and improving product quality. Advanced tools also enable manufacturers to detect and address issues early in the production process, leading to more efficient operations. 

 

**Medical Industry**

The medical industry relies heavily on precision measurement for the production of medical devices and equipment. Advanced measurement tools ensure that these devices meet stringent standards, ensuring patient safety and efficacy. Tools like the QuantuMike are essential in the development and quality control of medical instruments, contributing to advancements in healthcare technology. 

 

**Future Trends in Precision Measurement Technology**

The future of precision measurement technology is promising, with ongoing innovations aimed at further improving accuracy and efficiency. Emerging trends include the integration of artificial intelligence and machine learning, which can enhance predictive maintenance and quality control. Additionally, the development of more compact and portable measurement tools will expand their applications across various industries. 

 

**Conclusion**

Precision measurement technology continues to advance, driven by the need for greater accuracy and efficiency. Tools like the Mitutoyo QuantuMike exemplify these advancements, offering unparalleled precision and reliability. As industries continue to evolve, the role of precision measurement will only become more critical, driving innovation and ensuring quality in countless applications.

# The rise of smart sensors in precision metrology systems

![](https://secure.gravatar.com/avatar/edee3e53a71408f63ff6c253968c44eba0e61e14fd31406fedea74bbc4d87712?s=48&r=g)
![A metrological system](https://www.iotinsider.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/Metrological-systems-v2.png)

*Ellie Gabel*, *Associate Editor at Revolutionized, shares how smart sensors are shaping precision metrology systems*

The Internet of Things (IoT) and artificial intelligence (AI) have seeped into every industry, advancing technological precision. [Metrological systems](https://www.iotinsider.com/iot-insights/how-metrology-is-driving-medtech-innovation/) can obtain accurate measurements by leveraging smart sensors in everything from coordinate measuring machines (CMMs) to interferometrics.

Smart metrology’s impact is extensive, extending into automotive and aerospace. How is this equipment applied in specific fields, such as energy, manufacturing, and health care applications and innovations?

### Energy

Advanced metrology computes temperature or pressure changes in energy technologies, including pipes or solar panels. Governments need advanced metrology to power smart grids and protect them from cybersecurity threats. It will also better regulate measurements in retrofitted electrical delivery systems as the grid modernises.

Smart interferometrics and AI predict displacement shifts in energy infrastructure, suggesting maintenance before a pipeline could crack. Sensors customise and adjust flow control for oil and natural gas or grease lubrication in hydropower turbines.

The remote monitoring potential is invaluable to energy professionals, especially for hard-to-access assets. Underground power lines, primarily those underwater, will receive comprehensive oversight with 3D scanners and CMMs boosted by smart sensors. Experts easily gather geometrical data as energy systems undergo environmental stressors and fluctuate in usage.

Advanced metrology will also help in equipment manufacturing. Crafting a precise blade for a wind turbine is more straightforward, with sensors auto-aligning specifications with imported power plant standards.

### Manufacturing

Industry 4.0 and 5.0 rely on smart sensors to gather data and improve collaborative abilities between machines and humans. This is critical for automation and quality control in manufacturing operations.

Equipment like optical measurement systems uses light and lasers to measure components as they pass through production lines. Sensors with computer vision utilise digital images to compile the foundations of quality-checking software, improving the consistency of output and reducing the possibility of defects.

The image’s data informs algorithms, educating technicians on inspecting and calibrating parts to make better products. Technicians must measure thicknesses with numbers down to at least 0.001 when using precision devices like micrometers to promise quality functionality and accuracy.

External programs and smart metrology systems are rising because manufacturers and supply chains know they close skill gaps. Having visual references informs product assessments and preventive maintenance. Operators become intimately familiar with machine specifications and can report their health promptly and consistently. This also reduces the chances of safety incidents when machines work more efficiently with fewer faults.

### Healthcare

Advanced metrology provides numerous insights for histologists, imaging teams, surgeons and all medical professionals. Next-generation equipment, such as surface profilometers and computed tomography, benefits medical devices and research. It will also advance markets like wearables, taking accurate measurements for remote observation and spotting anomalies in biomarkers.

Microscopic blood or tissue samples are more straightforward to analyse with smart algorithms. Sensors deliver detailed results about textures and roughness, leading to exact diagnostics. The information may also fill knowledge gaps in research, providing nuance to fields with minimal studies by expanding the metadata.

Computed tomography scanners and X-rays require smart sensors to develop detailed images and models. Additionally, interoperability with other programs, like AI-informed software, could generate determinations about the pictures to curate targeted treatment plans based on patient history.

Medical facilities need these smart sensors to keep up with demand and the ever-changing landscape of disease development. The scaling potential of smart precision metrology is essential for faster patient care and research and development. The dimensional analysis is more meticulous, saving medical staff countless hours of labour and reducing human error.

### Precise measurements

The rise of smart sensor technology in metrology empowers industries toward better automation, remote monitoring and data integration practices. Precision metrology will only advance by adopting these systems. Products will become more refined and durable, research will become increasingly holistic, and automation will be the new norm.

Previously, metrology only provided information about measurements in practice. Now, smart sensors make them capable of collecting data and integrating with other devices, pushing industries into the next era of digitalisation.

*Author: Ellie Gabel, Associate Editor at [Revolutionized](https://revolutionized.com/)*

*There’s plenty of other editorial on our sister site, [Electronic Specifier](https://www.electronicspecifier.com/)! Or you can always join in the conversation by commenting below or visiting [our LinkedIn page.](https://www.linkedin.com/company/iot-insider/)*

SUMMARY:
Metrology 4.0 is revolutionizing manufacturing measurement by integrating advanced technologies which are enhancing precision, efficiency driving significant

URL: https://img.freepik.com/premium-photo/leveraging-quantum-principles-highly-precise-measurements-quantum-metrology-concept-quantum-physics-precision-measurements-quantum-technology-metrology-quantum-mechanics_918839-102907.jpg?w=2000
DESCRIPTION: A high-tech laser setup with vibrant blue, purple, orange, and yellow lighting, featuring metallic components and laser sparks, illustrating advanced precision metrology technology.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

enriched_points.expanded_content: Current precision metrology employs advanced tools like quantum lasers, smart sensors, and digital measurement systems, enhancing accuracy in industries from manufacturing to healthcare.
The precision metrology industry encounters challenges such as integrating new technologies, maintaining accuracy amidst evolving standards, and navigating market fluctuations affecting investment.

Continuous improvement in precision metrology is vital for enhancing efficiency, reducing errors, and fostering innovation, ensuring industries remain competitive in a rapidly advancing technological landscape.
